flags.concern: Does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure: Recurring debt: the save/poll interaction guard still omits the `useTimelineState.ts`-level plumbing needed to create a shared interaction ref before `useTimelineSave()` runs.
flags.evidence: Expanded scope to address all 13 open flags: (1) added useTimelineTrackManagement.ts as a full step covering moveClipToRow, createTrackAndMoveClip, keyboard moves, and batch moves; (2) moved interactionStateRef ownership from TimelineEditor.tsx up into useTimelineState.ts so useTimelineSave/persistence/poll can consume it; (3) committed to the preserve-don't-rebuild auto-sync policy in usePinnedShotGroups with rationale; (4) specified how the clip session is extended with pairClipId/pairBoundary fields to absorb the group-child-boundary session without losing two-clip context; (5) enumerated all three useClipDrag caller branches and made the new-track branch a pure rows+trackId update with no pinnedShotGroupsOverride; (6) added rowsToConfig/clonePinnedShotGroups in timeline-data.ts plus render-bounds.validation.test.ts to the shape-strip pass; (7) enumerated the broader test surface (useTimelineTrackManagement, useExternalDrop, useClipEditing, useClipDrag, render-bounds.validation, serialize, timeline-save-utils); (8) flagged the geometry-bearing callers in usePinnedShotGroups and useSwitchToFinalVideo for signature changes. Restructured into 5 phases / 15 steps. No scope creep beyond the rollback — all additions are in the video-editor tool and trace back to projection-era code paths.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Verified useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508 is the concrete test that asserts the auto-restore contract the plan's Step 6.4 now drops ('restores image snapshots inline when a pinned video clip is deleted'). Step 15.3 says to update tests that asserted the reconciler's auto-restore, and Step 15's file list names useClipEditing.test.ts, but the test case description in the plan is vague enough that the executor may only strip `start`/`children` fields instead of rewriting the assertion to the new contract (group preserved with empty `clipIds`, no clips reinserted). The plan should cite useClipEditing.test.ts:440 by name.
flags.evidence: Targeted iteration v5. (1) Step 5.1 explicitly adds `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` to the delete list, citing useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:357 as the sole remaining caller and pointing at Step 11.2 for the rewrite. (2) Step 11.2 now covers BOTH directions of useSwitchToFinalVideo: forward switchToFinalVideo (:81-123) and the explicit user-invocable switchToImages (:318-412). The reverse path is preserved with a concrete soft-tag rewrite spec: anchor = videoAction.start (line :334), insert image clips with cumulative offsets via getClipDuration, delete video clip in the same rows commit, soft-tag pinnedShotGroupsOverride, no updatePinnedGroupWithChildren import. Step 6.4 explicitly clarifies that "drop auto-restore" applies ONLY to the hidden reconciler path, not to switchToImages. (3) Step 15.2 explicitly names useClipEditing.test.ts:440-508 and :510-559 by line number with disposition "delete the entire it(...) block" and points at replacement coverage. (4) Step 15.6 adds a switchToImages regression test. (5) Step 16.1 and Validation Order step 4 explicitly call out the switchToImages flow. No scope growth — all additions are inside files already named in the plan.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 5.1 claims `updatePinnedGroupWithChildren` is 'currently imported by useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:357'. Verified at useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts:71-130 that this function is actually **defined locally** in useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts, not imported from pinned-group-projection.ts. The real imports from pinned-group-projection at :5-8 are `buildGroupFromChildren` and `replaceGroupChildren`, both of which are already in Step 5's delete list. The plan's characterization is factually wrong (delete list shouldn't include updatePinnedGroupWithChildren as a pinned-group-projection export), but the substantive intent still works: once `buildGroupFromChildren`/`replaceGroupChildren` are deleted, the local helper won't compile and Step 11.2's 'inline soft-tag construction' rewrite removes it. Minor wording error, not a blocking issue.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 11.2 specifies the reverse switch is 'Single commit, no `start`/`children`/`offset` reads anywhere' but doesn't state the mutation type explicitly. Since the step describes metaDeletes + clipOrderOverride + pinnedShotGroupsOverride in a single commit, the mutation must be `type: 'rows'` (the only variant carrying all three). Self-consistent with Step 11.1's restriction on `type: 'pinnedShotGroups'`, but making the mutation type explicit would reduce executor ambiguity.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Verified that `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` is a separate projection site that the plan does not explicitly call out. It is part of useTimelineCommit.ts (which the plan does name in Step 10), but the plan's Step 10 focus is the `pinnedShotGroups` mutation type, not the reconciler that runs during every `rows` mutation. The broader structural problem (cohesion-by-reprojection) lives in more places than Step 10 currently describes.
flags.evidence: Targeted iteration. (1) issue_hints-1, scope-1, all_locations, FLAG-301 — added a new dedicated Step 6 that names `reconcilePinnedShotGroupsInRows()` at useTimelineCommit.ts:110-255 and commits to **deleting it entirely**, plus updates `materializeData()` to drop the reconciler call. Verified the function exists and matches the critique. (2) issue_hints-2 — committed to **dropping** the reverse auto-restore behavior at :172-246 (delete a video-mode group's video clip → group is left empty, undo is the recovery path). Documented as a deliberate product change with rationale that it's exactly the kind of hidden reverse coupling the rollback eliminates. (3) scope-2 — explicit delete-shot ↔ reconciler coupling: delete-shot now deletes clips and entry in one commit with no auto-restore, stated in Step 6.5 and Step 11.3. (4) correctness — Step 9.2 now includes the resolveOverlaps caveat: same-track grouped drag re-derives clipIds from post-resolve order and includes a `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in the same `rows` commit if the order changed. (5) Step 13 now explicitly renders empty groups as a degraded placeholder instead of filtering them out, which is the rendering side of dropping auto-restore. Renumbered to 16 steps. No scope growth — the new Step 6 is inside `useTimelineCommit.ts` which was already named.

flags.concern: are the proposed changes technically correct?: Recurring debt: the new-track grouped drag path and createTrackAndMoveClip() cannot be rewritten to emit only type:'rows' because track creation mutates resolvedConfig.tracks[], which TimelineEditMutation.rows does not carry. Step 8/Step 9 say 'no pinnedShotGroupsOverride/resolved-config branch anymore' and 'one rows commit', but the plan must either keep a type:'config' branch with a soft-tag pinnedShotGroupsOverride shape, or extend TimelineEditMutation.rows with a tracks override. Leaving this ambiguous risks either losing the new track or splitting one action across two commits.
flags.evidence: Targeted iteration. (1) FLAG-201/correctness/callers — committed to keeping `type: 'config'` for the new-track grouped-drag branch (useClipDrag.ts:430-463) and createTrackAndMoveClip() (useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:191-217), since track creation mutates resolvedConfig.tracks[] and TimelineEditMutation.rows has no tracks field (verified at useTimelineCommit.ts:48-65). The override is constrained to the soft-tag shape (no start/children) and the type is tightened in useTimelineCommit so geometry can't leak back in. Same-track grouped drags still use `rows`. The action stays in one commit. (2) issue_hints — Step 11 now explicitly states the reorder-only case is a deliberate no-op on the timeline, with rationale and a code-comment requirement. (3) all_locations — committed to deleting pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts in Step 5 and explicitly enumerated it in Step 14. No scope growth; if anything Step 8/9 are slightly narrower because they no longer claim to remove the config branch.

flags.concern: did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: the committed preserve-timing auto-sync policy in Step 11 does not specify what happens when the underlying shot's image order changes but membership is unchanged. The plan says surviving members keep at/hold exactly and clipIds is re-sorted by live at, which means image reordering in the shot panel has zero effect on the pinned group — a plausible choice but one that differs from current behavior and should be stated explicitly.
flags.evidence: Plan Step 11 lists 'reorder (no clip-timing change)' as a test case without stating the expected storage/timeline outcome; current auto-sync in usePinnedShotGroups.ts:231-453 does reorder.

flags.concern: does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts:330 wires an isInteractionActive harness into surviving helpers and is only covered by Step 5's 'rewrite or delete' without a commitment. flags.evidence: src/tools/video-editor/lib/pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts:330 passes isInteractionActive: () => resizeActive into helpers the plan is reshaping.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Step 11 now commits to an explicit preserve-don't-rebuild auto-sync policy with rationale, resolving issue_hints-2. flags.concern: did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: the reconciler at useTimelineCommit.ts:172-230 implements an undocumented product behavior: when a video-mode group's video clip is deleted, it auto-restores the original image clips from `imageClipSnapshot` at `group.start`. Plan Step 10 addresses useSwitchToFinalVideo's forward direction but is silent on this reverse restore-on-delete path. The plan should explicitly state whether this behavior is preserved under the soft-tag model (and how — there is no `group.start` to anchor the restored clips) or intentionally dropped.
flags.evidence: useTimelineCommit.ts:172-230 branches on `group.imageClipSnapshot?.length` when a video group's clip is gone and rebuilds the image actions using `let cursor = group.start`.

flags.concern: are the proposed changes technically correct?: Step 8.2 says same-track grouped drag can emit `type: 'rows'` with 'No pinnedShotGroupsOverride needed since clipIds order is unchanged on a same-row translate.' That is true for a pure translate with no collisions but `resolveOverlaps` can reorder clips during a drag if they collide with other clips on the row. The executor should still re-sort `clipIds` by live `at` and emit a `pinnedShotGroupsOverride` in that collision case. Minor, but worth calling out so the executor does not hard-code the 'no override' path.
flags.evidence: src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts reorders clip positions during drag commits; Step 8.2 unconditionally says pinnedShotGroupsOverride is not needed.
